title: Policy and the branches of government
description: Test your knowledge on how the other branches use formal and informal powers to hold the bureaucracy accountable!
topic_path: Policy and the branches of government | 2.15
Policy and the branches of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best explains a limit to the Court’s powers over bureaucratic agencies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The bureaucracy can ignore or refuse to implement court rulings.
The president can overrule the Court’s decision.
Their decisions require final approval from Congress.
They can only stop presidential actions, not bureaucratic actions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes one limitation that Congress faces when attempting to monitor the bureaucracy directly by calling oversight hearings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Oversight hearings take a lot of time and resources to be effective and Congress is too small to monitor all administrative actions directly.
The president has the power to cancel any oversight hearings on the agenda if they feel like it will interfere with the agency’s ability to implement policy.
Congressional hearings have no legal standing and Congress has no powers to force bureaucrats to testify before committees.
The separation of powers gives the judicial branch the power to hold hearings, not the legislative branch.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes one obstacle the president faces when trying to manage the bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The bureaucracy is large and handles many issues, making it difficult for the president to stay informed on all administrative activities.
The bureaucracy has a stronger relationship with Congress, making them less obligated to the president and their goals.
The bureaucracy is in charge of implementing policy, which falls outside of the scope of the tasks under the presidency.
The bureaucracy has a lot of turnover where employees constantly leave and the president has to replace them.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2016, Congress passed a law that intended to improve transportation in rural areas, relying on the Department of Transportation to figure out the strongest mode of transportation. Senator Tahani Al-Jamil has heard that the Department of Transportation is planning to institute a regulation requiring state governments to give every person living in a rural area a brand-new car, which was not the intent of the law. However, she’s not sure if this is gossip or an actual plan. 

**Which of the following is an informal power that Senator Al-Jamil could use to get information about how the Department of Transportation is implementing the law?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She could reach out to her contacts in the Department of Transportation to find out more about their implementation plans.
She could call a Senate hearing and request the head of the Department of Transportation testify before the Senate.
She could propose new legislation limiting the kinds of regulations that the Department of Transportation can make.
She could reduce the amount of funding the Department of Transportation gets for the next year to stop them from implementing the plan.

assessment_items.question_content:
Congress passed a law that tasked the Department of Homeland Security with developing an emergency communication system to alert residents. The department has defined emergency communications as information important to most citizens. Under that definition, the head of Homeland Security has used the system to send out spoilers for the tv show, *Derry Girls.* 

**What formal action could Congress take to limit the agency’s discretionary authority and hold the bureaucracy accountable?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pass legislation to narrow the definition of what it considers an “emergency”
fire and replace the head of the Department of Homeland Security
leverage interest groups to pressure the department to stop sending out spoilers
ask the Supreme Court to decide if this action oversteps the intent of the law

assessment_items.question_content:
The Federal Election Commission enforces federal election laws. However, they have not filled an open position in four years, preventing the commission from enforcing laws. In response, the Senate Committee on Rules and Administration calls a hearing and subpoenas the members of the Federal Election Committee to testify. 

**Which of the following statements best explains the reason the Senate committee called this hearing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to investigate why the FEC is not enforcing laws and hold the bureaucracy accountable
to pressure the president to eliminate the FEC by drawing national attention to the situation
to limit the FEC’s rule-making authority so it will enforce the established rules
to change all election laws that the FEC enforces and return electioneering power to the states

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of how the president can use informal powers to limit a bureaucratic agency’s focus when implementing a policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president attaches a signing statement to a law explaining how their administration should implement it.
The president replaces the head of a bureaucratic agency with someone more closely aligned to their ideology.
The president uses their bully pulpit to support a law making its way through Congress.
The president proposes a national budget that increases funding for a bureaucratic agency.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios is an example of how the president can hold a bureaucratic agency accountable?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president issued an executive order to split the Department of Veterans Affairs into two agencies after the organization failed to meet policy goals.
The president receives a briefing from their chief of staff on the current actions of all bureaucratic agencies.
The president nominates a strong campaign supporter to Secretary of Education after being elected to office.
The president lists their policy goals in the annual State of the Union address to the other branches of the federal government.

assessment_items.question_content:
Congress passed a law ordering the Department of Agriculture to create a National School Lunch Program that provides nutritionally-balanced meals to all students for free. President Stanley Hudson attached a statement when signing the bill into law, stating that his administration should include soft pretzels in their nutritionally-balanced meal plans. 

**Which of the following statements best explains how a signing statement affects the Department of Agriculture’s implementation of the policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It guides the rules and regulations that the Department of Agriculture issues to implement policy, especially when the law is ambiguous.
It limits the Department of Agriculture’s rule-making authority to implement the law to the best of its ability.
It shifts the responsibility of implementing a policy to the experts within the Department of Agriculture.
It can check the power of the bureaucracy to implement policy in a way that conflicts with the president’s ideology.

assessment_items.question_content:
Under President Jean-Ralphio Saperstein, the Department of the Interior issued a new rule allowing private businesses to buy sections of land designated for national parks. In response, several environmental groups band together and sue the federal government to stop the implementation of the rule. The case makes its way to a federal court of appeals. 

**Which of the following actions could the federal court take to stop the Department of the Interior from implementing the rule?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: order the department to stop
hold an investigative hearing
stop funding the department
replace the Secretary of the Interior

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which of the following situations would a federal court most likely declare a bureaucratic agency’s actions void?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when the actions extend beyond the intent of the law passed by Congress
when the executive and legislative branches are controlled by different parties
when the legislative branch is gridlocked and refusing to pass new legislation
when a state refuses to follow the regulations set by the federal bureaucracy

assessment_items.question_content:
To protect young children from obscene content, Congress passed a law giving the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) the authority to make rules limiting what television networks can broadcast. In response, the FCC makes a rule that television networks can only broadcast shows safe for people of all ages, fining any network that shows programs made for mature audiences. The National Association of Broadcasters sued the FCC Chairman, and the case makes its way to the Supreme Court.

**Which of the following measures can the Supreme Court use to limit the FCC’s actions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: overrule the FCC and strike down the administrative action for overextending beyond the intent of the law
impeach the FCC chairman and order the president to nominate someone new
use their power of judicial review to rule that the law passed by Congress is unconstitutional
make a new regulation allowing television programs to show programs for mature audiences after dark

assessment_items.question_content:
A national parent-teacher organization is concerned about the effects of high-caffeine energy drinks on teenagers. To influence policy, the group testifies before a congressional committee, submits a formal request to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to review current labeling requirements, and organizes a campaign to educate families and school communities.

**What does this scenario best illustrate the structure of the U.S. government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Citizens can influence the policymaking process at multiple access points.
Advocacy groups must rely on Congress alone to enact public health reforms.
The president can block regulatory changes without input from institutions.
Congressional committees are required to adopt all agency recommendations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is an accurate comparison of how the legislative and judicial branches provide access points to influence policy?**

|  | **Legislative Branch** | **Judicial Branch** |
| - | - | - |
| A | Overrides presidential vetoes | Reviews executive orders before implementation |
| B | Holds public hearings on proposed laws | Interprets laws through constitutional rulings |
| C  | Introduces and debates bills | Enforces laws passed by Congress |
| D | Allocates funding to federal agencies | Appoints lower court judges |


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is it often difficult for a single branch of the federal government to create and implement national policy on its own?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Because federal lawmaking usually requires agreement between Congress and the president
Because the Constitution prevents the executive branch from proposing policy ideas
Because the Supreme Court frequently initiates national policy changes
Because members of Congress must seek approval from the judicial branch before passing laws

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the best explanation for why sharing power among the three branches often constrains national policymaking?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Laws passed by Congress can be limited or overturned by the courts.
Each branch operates independently and coordination leads to gridlock.
Laws may be passed when all three branches agree on principles of government.
The president may use executive orders to act when Congress fails to pass legislation.

assessment_items.question_content:
In *United States v. Windsor* (2013), the Supreme Court struck down a section of the federal Defense of Marriage Act (DOMA) that defined marriage exclusively as a union between one man and one woman. The Court said that DOMA violated the Constitution because it treated same-sex couples unfairly. After the decision, the federal government had to recognize same-sex marriages that were legal under state law. This gave married same-sex couples access to federal benefits like tax breaks and Social Security.

**What does this case demonstrate about the judiciary’s role in policymaking?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Constitutional rulings by the Supreme Court can lead to significant changes in federal policy.
The judiciary enforces public policy created by Congress and the president.
The Court’s influence is limited to symbolic decisions without legal consequences.
Courts must wait for legislative approval before addressing constitutional questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
A president proposes major immigration reform, which passes in the House of Representatives but fails to advance in the Senate. In response, the president issues an executive order to alter how existing immigration laws are enforced. A federal court later blocks the order, ruling that it exceeds the president’s constitutional authority. The administration appeals the ruling, setting the stage for a higher court to determine the limits of executive power in immigration policy.

**What statement best illustrates the principle demonstrated by this scenario?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The policymaking process is shaped by checks and balances.
Congress exercises exclusive control over immigration policy.
The separation of powers enables each branch to act without interference.
Executive actions are not subject to review by the judicial branch.
